title: Tanzania Passports and Travel Document Regulations, 2004
title_en: Tanzania Passports and Travel Document Regulations, 2004

provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Tanzania Passports and Travel Document Regulations, 2004.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires— " Act " means the Passports and Travel Documents Act, 2002; " Director " means the Director of Immigration Services; " The East African Community " means the community established by Article 20, the treaty for the establishment of the East African Community 1999, comprising the United Republic of Tanzania, The Republic of Kenya and The Republic of Uganda; " Issuing Authority " means the person authorized by the Act to issue Passports and Travel Documents; " Minister " means the Minister responsible for Home Affairs. Part II – Issuance of passports and travel documents

provisions.title: Issuing centers
provisions.content: (1) Passports and Travel Documents shall be issued at the Immigration Department Headquarters in Dar es Salaam and in Zanzibar. (2) The Director may designate any other issuing centers in the Regions or Diplomatic Missions abroad as the circumstances may allow. Part III – Application procedures, forms, cards and fees

provisions.title: Procedure for application for new Passports and Travel Documents
provisions.content: (1) Application for a new Passport and Travel Document shall be lodged by the applicant at the Immigration Headquarters, Zanzibar and any Regional or District Immigration office where the applicant resides. (2) The Director may, if there are reasonable grounds to do so waive the requirements to lodge the application at the Regional or District Immigration Office. (3) Upon submission of the application for a new passport the applicant shall be required to report to a designated police station and fill in a prescribed form for finger prints: Provided that the Director may upon reasonable circumstances waive the requirement of finger prints.

provisions.title: Application procedure for replacement of Passports and Travel document
provisions.content: (1) Application for replacement of Passport and Travel Document which has no further pages for endorsement or has expired shall be lodged by the Applicant at the Immigration headquarters, Zanzibar and any Regional or District Immigration office where the Applicant resides. (2) The Director may waive the requirements to lodge reapplication of a new Passport at the Regional or District Office where there are reasonable grounds to do so.

provisions.title: Documents to be submitted to support application for a new Passport
provisions.content: Apart from the requirements provided for under section 12(2) of the Act , the applicant, shall be required to submit the following documents to support the application for a Passport for a specified trip. (a) Private trips— The application for private trips shall be accompanied by— (i) Proof of applicant's activities; (ii) Invitation letter; (iii) Return ticket; (iv) Permission letter from the parent or guardian to the applicant below the age of 18; (v) Permission letter from the employer if the applicant is employed; or (vi) Letter from Ward Executive Officer/Sheha where the applicant resides. (b) Employment trips— The application for employment trips shall be accompanied by— (i) Offer of employment; (ii) Return ticket; and (iii) Letter from Ward Executive Officer/Sheha where the applicant resides. (c) Study trips— The application for study trips shall be accompanied by— (i) Admission letter; (ii) Academic certificates; (iii) Sponsorship letter; (iv) Proof of payment for private sponsorship; (v) Permission letter from the Employer if the applicant is employed; and (vi) Permission letter from parent or guardian to the applicant below age 18. (d) Religious trips— The application for religious trips shall be accompanied by— (i) Invitation letter; and (ii) A letter from a designated registered Religious organization responsible for the trip. (e) Medical treatment Trips— The application for medical treatment trips shall be accompanied by (i) A letter from the Principal Medical Officer; or (ii) Proof from a registered Medical Practitioner; and (iii) A letter of authority from the Ministry of Health. (f) Sports trips— The application for sports trips shall be accompanied by— (i) A letter from the Tanzania Football Association (FAT) or the Zanzibar Football Association (ZFA); or (ii) A letter from any registered sports association; and (iii) Invitation letter. (g) Scouts trips— The application for Scouts trips shall be accompanied by— (i) A letter from a relevant Scouts association; (ii) Permission letter from the parent or guardian to the applicant below the age of 18; and (iii) Invitation letter. (h) Official trips— The application for official trips shall be accompanied by— (i) A letter from the employer; (ii) Invitation letter; and (iii) Identity card of the applicant. (i) Business trips— The application for business trips shall be accompanied by— (i) A valid business licence; and (ii) Return ticket. (j) Seaman trips— The application for seaman trips shall be accompanied by— (i) Contract of employment; (ii) Seaman discharge book; (iii) Academic certificate; and (iv) Letter from a relevant seamen Union.

provisions.title: Documents to be submitted to support the application for Travel Documents
provisions.content: (1) The application for an emergency Travel Document shall be accompanied by four passport size photographs. (2) The application for a certificate of identity shall be accompanied by— (a) Police loss report, where there are no Embassy office; and (b) Two passport size photographs. (3) The application for a Geneva Convention travel document shall be accompanied by— (a) A letter from the Director of Refugees Department; (b) Copy of refugee Identity card; and (c) Two passport size photos.

provisions.title: Documents to support application for replacement of Passport and travel document
provisions.content: (1) The application for replacement of a passport shall be accompanied by— (a) The previous passport; and (b) Four passport size photographs. (2) The application for replacement of a travel document shall be accompanied by— (a) The previous travel document; and (b) Two passport size photos.

provisions.title: Forms and cards
provisions.content: (1) The forms and cards set out in the First Schedule to these Regulation shall be used for the purposes to which they refer and are capable of being adapted and such forms and cards with such modifications as may be necessary are hereby prescribed for the purpose of the Act and these Regulations.

provisions.title: Fees
provisions.content: (1) There shall be paid in respect of a passport and travel document provided for under the first column of the Second Schedule to these Regulations the fees set out opposite thereto in the second column. (2) The Minister may from time to time vary the fees or rates provided for under sub-regulation (1) Above as he may determine. Part IV – Validity of Passport and Travel Documents

provisions.title: Validity of Passports and Travel Documents Act
provisions.content: (1) Passports and travel documents shall be valid for the period provided for under section 8 of the Act . (2) Subject to the provisions of the Act and these Regulations relating to passports or any travel documents the Director may vary the period of validity of any Passport or Travel Document to be issued to, any person as he may deem fit. Part V – Revocation of Passports and Travel Documents

provisions.title: Revocation of passports and Travel Documents
provisions.content: (1) The Director shall revoke any Passport or Travel Document in accordance with section 17 of the Act as provided for under the Third Schedule of these Regulations. (2) The Director shall on revocation require the holder of the passport or Travel Document as the case may be to surrender the same with a period not exceeding thirty days from the date of notification. (3) The Director shall revoke a Passport or Travel Document as the case may be after the expiry of ninety days from the date of surrender whether there was an appeal or not. (4) Upon surrendering of the Passport or Travel Document as the case may, within a period of thirty days be in accordance with section 17(3) of the Act , the holder may appeal to the Minister if he so wishes pursuant to the provisions of section 18 of the Act . (5) The Minister shall, upon receipt of the appeal, make his decision within a period of sixty days and his decision shall be final. (6) The decision of the Minister shall be communicated to the appellant and the Director within fourteen days from the date of the decision. (7) Where the Minister makes decision in favour of the appellant and the same communicated to the Director , the Director shall restore to the appellant the Passport or Travel Document as the case may be or reissue another passport or travel document. (8) Where the appellant is re-issued with a new Passport or Travel Document under sub-regulation (2) he shall pay the prescribed fee. (9) Any person who fails to comply with sub-regulation (2) commits an offence and upon conviction shall be liable to a fine of not less than one hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a term of one year or both such fine and imprisonment. First Schedule Forms [Form CT5(Ai), previously form CT5(A) renumbered and substituted by section 2 of Government Notice 7 of 2006 ] [Form CTA(ii) inserted by section 2 of Government Notice 7 of 2006 ] [Form CTA(iii) inserted by section 2 of Government Notice 7 of 2006 ] [Editorial note: The forms have not been reproduced.] Second Schedule Fees Ordinary Passport 50,000/= Diplomatic Passport 50,000/= Service Passport 50,000/= East African Passport 15,000/= Emergency Travel Document 10,000/= Certificate of Identity 10,000/= Geneva Convention Travel Document 20,000/= Third Schedule (Made under Regulation 6) Reviction notice [Editorial note: The form has not been reproduced.] Fourth Schedule Certificate of identity [Editorial note: The form has not been reproduced.] History of this document 01 February 2018 amendment not yet applied Amended by Tanzania Passports and Travel Documents (Amendment) Regulations, 2018 20 January 2006 this version Amended by Tanzania Passports and Travel Documents (Amendment) Regulations, 2006 11 June 2004 Published in Gazette of the United Republic of Tanzania 24 Commenced Read this version Related documents Is amended by Tanzania Passports and Travel Documents (Amendment) Regulations, 2006 Tanzania Passports and Travel Documents (Amendment) Regulations, 2018 More documents like this one [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Twitter/X profile Visit our Linked In profile Visit our YouTube channel About TanzLII TanzLII is operated by the Judiciary of Tanzania and the Office of the Attorney General, Chief Parliamentary Draftsman, and publishes the law of Tanzania for free online access for all.
